steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which verb form conforms to Standard English. The skill is parallel structure (verb-form agreement within a compound predicate). The decisive clue is the verb "premiered," which is past tense — the blank is the second verb joined to it by "and," so it must also be simple past tense to match.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "developing": A present participle cannot serve as the second verb in a compound predicate alongside a simple past verb; "premiered... and developing" violates parallel structure. ✗
  • Choice B — "developed": Simple past tense matches "premiered," forming a grammatically parallel compound predicate: "premiered... and developed." ✓
  • Choice C — "to develop": An infinitive cannot function as a parallel verb in a compound predicate; "premiered... and to develop" violates parallel structure. ✗
  • Choice D — "having developed": A perfect participle cannot serve as the second verb in a compound predicate alongside a simple past verb; "premiered... and having developed" violates parallel structure. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
